original_query: select  c_customer_id as customer_id        , coalesce(c_last_name,'') || ', ' || coalesce(c_first_name,'') as customername  from customer      ,customer_address      ,customer_demographics      ,household_demographics      ,income_band      ,store_returns  where ca_city	        =  'Hopewell'    and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk    and ib_lower_bound   >=  32287    and ib_upper_bound   <=  32287 + 50000    and ib_income_band_sk = hd_income_band_sk    and cd_demo_sk = c_current_cdemo_sk    and hd_demo_sk = c_current_hdemo_sk    and sr_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk  order by c_customer_id  limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_address AS (
    SELECT ca_address_sk
    FROM customer_address
    WHERE ca_city = 'Hopewell'
),
filtered_income AS (
    SELECT ib_income_band_sk
    FROM income_band
    WHERE ib_lower_bound >= 32287
      AND ib_upper_bound <= 82287
)
SELECT c_customer_id AS customer_id,
       COALESCE(c_last_name, '') || ', ' || COALESCE(c_first_name, '') AS customername
FROM customer c
JOIN filtered_address fa ON c.c_current_addr_sk = fa.ca_address_sk
JOIN household_demographics hd ON c.c_current_hdemo_sk = hd.hd_demo_sk
JOIN filtered_income fi ON hd.hd_income_band_sk = fi.ib_income_band_sk
JOIN customer_demographics cd ON c.c_current_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk
JOIN store_returns sr ON cd.cd_demo_sk = sr.sr_cdemo_sk
ORDER BY c_customer_id
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replace implicit comma-based joins with explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and query structure.
Replace the calculation '32287 + 50000' with the constant value '82287' in the ib_upper_bound condition.
